project_structure: Bootstrap templates live in two orthogonal locations that have drifted: (1) packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ contains three orphaned hand-edited files (CLAUDE.md, codex-AGENTS-header.md, cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc) that are NOT consumed by any production source under packages/cli/src/; (2) the actual install path uses packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection() which writes a single HTML-comment-wrapped managed section (marker `fabric:knowledge-base`) into the three SECTION_TARGETS files: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules. A fourth file packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template is also orphaned (no consumer in src/; its content overlaps with the AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT inline string at install.ts:259). The repo's own .fabric/AGENTS.md (the rc.19 future canonical source) does not yet exist — only the root AGENTS.md carries the managed block today.

relevant_files.rationale: One of three legacy bootstrap templates rc.19 plans to consolidate. Currently contains 7 lines of behavior rules in Chinese and a dead pointer to `.fabric/bootstrap/README.md` (line 2), plus `@AGENTS.md` import directive (line 8). NOT consumed by any code in packages/cli/src/ — confirmed via `grep -r templates/bootstrap packages/cli/src/` returning empty.

key_code.description: `- 主说明文档已收敛到 \`.fabric/bootstrap/README.md\`。` — dead pointer; .fabric/bootstrap/ no longer exists per v2.0 (server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:555 comment 'v2.0: no special-cased L0 node').
`修改任何文件前必须调用 fab_plan_context(paths=[<被改文件>]), 再调用 fab_get_knowledge_sections...` — this rule is the canonical behavior rule that rc.19 will re-state inside .fabric/AGENTS.md.
`@AGENTS.md` — Claude Code import syntax. In rc.19 thin-shell model, this becomes `@.fabric/AGENTS.md`.

relevant_files.topic_relation: bootstrap-templates exploration must capture the exact byte content of all three orphaned templates so rc.19 can decide what to keep (behavior rules in .fabric/AGENTS.md), what to delete (dead `.fabric/bootstrap/README.md` line), and what to migrate (@AGENTS.md → @.fabric/AGENTS.md).

relevant_files.rationale: Second of three legacy bootstrap templates. Contains the same dead pointer + behavior rules pattern as CLAUDE.md but without the `@AGENTS.md` directive. NOT consumed by any code in packages/cli/src/.

key_code.description: `- 主说明文档已收敛到 \`.fabric/bootstrap/README.md\`。` — same dead pointer as CLAUDE.md; must be removed in rc.19.
`- 此 header 仅用于把 Codex 引导到 Fabric 项目级规则入口。` — meta-note about the file's role; obsolete because Codex now reads AGENTS.md directly with managed-block injection.

relevant_files.topic_relation: bootstrap-templates analysis confirms this file is also orphaned and should be deleted. Codex receives the same rc.19 bootstrap content as the other two clients via the consolidated managed-block writer pointing to .fabric/AGENTS.md.

relevant_files.rationale: Third of three legacy bootstrap templates. Only one with YAML front-matter (`alwaysApply: true`, `description:`). Reference for Cursor `.mdc` metadata pattern. NOT consumed by any code in packages/cli/src/.

key_code.description:
`---
alwaysApply: true
description: Fabric Protocol bootstrap rules
---` — Cursor's project-rules metadata. `alwaysApply: true` means Cursor injects the rule unconditionally on every prompt. Required for the rule to be active without `globs:` or `@` invocation.
`本项目使用 Fabric Protocol 管理规则。` — slightly different phrasing from CLAUDE.md/codex-AGENTS-header.md (Protocol vs. just Fabric). Demonstrates drift across the three templates.
Refers to `fab_get_rules` semantics implicitly via `fab_plan_context + fab_get_knowledge_sections`. Matches the new v2 knowledge-API verbs, but path resolution `<被改文件>` still uses pre-v2 phrasing.

relevant_files.topic_relation: bootstrap-templates exploration must specify the Cursor `.mdc` front-matter requirements for rc.19's `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` (or whatever filename is chosen). The thin-shell Cursor file MUST keep `alwaysApply: true` + `description:` lines BEFORE the managed-block markers so Cursor activates the rule.

relevant_files.rationale: Repo-root AGENTS.md is the ONLY file in this project that currently carries the `fabric:knowledge-base` managed block (verified L10-L21). Lines 1-8 are user-authored content (preserved across re-runs); lines 10-21 are the managed section. This is the existing pattern rc.19 will replicate for `.fabric/AGENTS.md` + new bootstrap files.

key_code.description: `<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin --> ... <!-- fabric:knowledge-base:end -->` wraps the Fabric Knowledge Base subsection (KB-only content, no behavior rules). rc.19 will rename marker to `fabric:bootstrap` and expand block to include behavior rules + KB section.

User-authored README content survives re-runs verbatim. For rc.19, this content is irrelevant once .fabric/AGENTS.md becomes the source; root AGENTS.md becomes a thin shell that just hosts the managed block.

relevant_files.topic_relation: bootstrap-templates angle: this file shows the working managed-block pattern (existing fabric:knowledge-base marker + atomic in-place replacement). rc.19's `fabric:bootstrap` marker is a rename + body-expansion of this exact pattern.

relevant_files.rationale: Source of truth for the current `fabric:knowledge-base` marker writer. Contains SECTION_TARGETS (L224), FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN/END_MARKER constants (L235-L236), FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX (L246), buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection() (L291-L304) and addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection() (L638-L702). rc.19 must rename the marker and expand the body builder.

key_code.description: `['CLAUDE.md', 'AGENTS.md', join('.cursor', 'rules')]` — note `.cursor/rules` is a single FILE path, NOT a directory of .mdc files. This is a pre-existing drift bug: real Cursor rules live at `.cursor/rules/<name>.mdc`. rc.19 should fix this to `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc`.
`<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin -->` — rc.19 renames to `<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin -->`. Marker is a plain string substituted into regex L246.

Non-greedy regex `/(?:\r?\n){0,2}<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin -->[\s\S]*?<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:end -->/` — must be updated in lockstep with marker rename. Used by both install (in-place replace) and uninstall (strip).

Template literal that emits the section body. Currently includes only KB discovery/usage/write-flows/language bullets. rc.19 expands this to include behavior rules section + KB section, with body = byte copy of .fabric/AGENTS.md (+ optional `\n---\n` + .fabric/project-rules.md).

Idempotent writer: if marker found, in-place replace via FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX; else append with one blank-line separator. Skips files that don't exist (won't create anchor files). rc.19 keeps the same writer mechanics but changes input source from string-literal to file-read of .fabric/AGENTS.md.

relevant_files.topic_relation: This file is the only place where bootstrap content is actually materialized today. The orphan templates in packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ never participate; everything the user sees comes from this writer's string-literal body. rc.19's consolidation MUST update this file to read .fabric/AGENTS.md instead of building the string in code.

relevant_files.rationale: Counterpart strip logic — imports SECTION_TARGETS, FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX, FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN/END_MARKER. Strips the managed region during `fab uninstall`. Must be updated in lockstep when rc.19 renames the marker.

key_code.description: Orchestrator that fans out marker-strip across SECTION_TARGETS. Inverse of addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection. rc.19 marker rename touches the imports here only — no logic changes needed.
Reads each SECTION_TARGETS path, applies FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX to strip the managed region (markers inclusive + optional leading blank line). Must keep working with renamed `fabric:bootstrap` marker.

relevant_files.topic_relation: bootstrap-templates rc.19 marker rename has a downstream effect: uninstall must strip the NEW marker name. If users installed under rc.18 (knowledge-base marker) then uninstall under rc.19 (bootstrap marker), the old marker would not be stripped. rc.19 plan must decide: support both markers in regex, or accept clean-slate drift (Y memory: pre-user clean-slate preference).

Also contains AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT (L259-L267) — the inline string used to create root AGENTS.md when missing (L730-L732). rc.19 changes: root AGENTS.md content shrinks to thin-shell (just the managed block); new .fabric/AGENTS.md created with the locked content.

Currently 8 lines of project-knowledge intro + Fabric reference. rc.19: this should become the thin-shell content (markers only, with optional pre-marker header) OR be deleted in favor of templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template (which is currently orphaned).

Idempotent guard: only writes AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT when plan.agentsMdAction === 'created' AND !existsSync(plan.agentsMdPath). rc.19 adds a parallel write for .fabric/AGENTS.md with the locked rc.19 content.
10-step stage: skill installs (3) + hook scripts (3) + hook libs (1) + hook config merges (3) + managed-section write (1). rc.19: section write at L1043 must use new file-reading body builder.

The bootstrap-stage orchestration list is the integration point where rc.19 inserts (a) .fabric/AGENTS.md creation if missing, (b) revised section body source.

`- 详细 bootstrap 说明位于 \`.fabric/bootstrap/README.md\`.` — same dead pointer as the three bootstrap/ templates. Must be removed.

relevant_files.topic_relation: bootstrap-templates rc.19 adds a new CHANGELOG entry — existing entries are historical and should NOT be edited.

patterns:
Existing managed-block pattern (rc.12 broad-gate-fabric-lang TASK-006): HTML-comment-wrapped section with begin/end markers, in-place replace via non-greedy regex, idempotent + byte-identical re-runs, skips absent files (does not create anchors), user content outside markers preserved verbatim, user content INSIDE markers intentionally overwritten on re-run. Section body built via template literal in buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection() with fabric_language interpolated. Marker constants centralized as `FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER` / `FABRIC_SECTION_END_MARKER` in skills-and-hooks.ts but DUPLICATED as local constants in test files (drift risk). Cursor `.mdc` front-matter pattern (from cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc): three-line YAML block at top — `---
alwaysApply: true
description: <text>
---` — required for the rule to activate. Test fixture pattern uses `.cursor/rules` as a FILE path (not directory), which is a pre-existing drift from real Cursor convention (`.cursor/rules/<name>.mdc`). Atomic write pattern via atomicWriteText() throughout — half-written-file failure mode is out of scope.
dependencies: Internal dependencies for the bootstrap section path: packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts (writer + constants) → consumed by packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts (full init) AND packages/cli/src/install/hooks-orchestrator.ts (standalone `fab hooks install`). Uninstall counterpart: packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts imports SECTION_TARGETS + FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX. Test imports: install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts + uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts DUPLICATE marker constants locally (drift risk). Schema dependencies: packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts contains dead `.fabric/bootstrap/README.md` special-case branches in stable_id + layer derivation (L223, L249). i18n dependencies: cli.scan.recommendation.init key in en.ts (L375) + zh-CN.ts (L369). No runtime/dynamic key lookup detected for bootstrap content — all paths are statically string-literal'd.
integration_points: rc.19 integration touch points: (1) packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts — rename markers (knowledge-base → bootstrap), update FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX in lockstep, change buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection signature/body to read from .fabric/AGENTS.md instead of inline template literal. (2) packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts:730-732 — add parallel write for .fabric/AGENTS.md with rc.19-locked content; AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT (L259) shrinks to thin-shell (markers + optional pre-marker header). (3) packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts — imports auto-track marker rename. (4) Tests: install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts (L442-L443, L465-L467) + uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts (L329-L330) update local marker constants and target paths. (5) Schemas: packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts (L223, L249) — remove dead special-case clauses or remap to `.fabric/AGENTS.md`. (6) i18n: en.ts:375 + zh-CN.ts:369 update. (7) Templates to DELETE: packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/{CLAUDE.md, codex-AGENTS-header.md, cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc} (orphans, no consumer). (8) Templates to DECIDE: packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template (also orphan; either wire in as the .fabric/AGENTS.md source or delete). (9) Docs cleanup: docs/SPEC_INTERNAL.md, docs/getting-started.md, docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md, docs/test-seed/server.md. (10) Config resolver: packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts:105/122/135/148 bootstrapTargetPath field — likely remove (no consumer).
constraints: Hard constraints from existing system: (1) Marker rename MUST be lockstep across writer regex, constant exports, uninstall strip regex, and test local copies — otherwise install/uninstall asymmetry breaks idempotency. (2) Cursor `.cursor/rules/<name>.mdc` files REQUIRE YAML front-matter (`alwaysApply: true` + `description:`) BEFORE the managed-block markers for the rule to activate in Cursor; the front-matter is the 'pre-managed-block content' that must survive re-runs. (3) Claude Code thin-shell `CLAUDE.md` can use `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` import directive, but Claude also reads AGENTS.md natively — duplicate content (CLAUDE.md @-import + root AGENTS.md managed block) is allowed but creates two activation paths. (4) Codex CLI reads root `AGENTS.md` directly; thin shell = root AGENTS.md with managed block. (5) Per memory `feedback_clean_slate.md`, while Fabric has zero users, prefer clean-slate marker rename (no dual-marker backward-compat in uninstall regex) over preserving rc.18 marker. (6) The `.cursor/rules` SECTION_TARGETS path is a FILE (existing tests + writer treat it as such); rc.19 must DECIDE: fix to `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` (proper Cursor convention) OR keep as `.cursor/rules` (matches existing tests, but Cursor docs deprecate this pattern). (7) The locked `.fabric/AGENTS.md` content uses `fab_plan_context` + `fab_get_knowledge_sections` verbs — these MUST match the actual MCP tool names registered by fabric-server (verify against tools registration in install-flow exploration).

clarification_needs.question: Should `.cursor/rules` SECTION_TARGETS path be migrated to `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` (proper Cursor directory-rules convention with .mdc extension + YAML front-matter) or kept as `.cursor/rules` (existing single-file convention matching current tests)?
clarification_needs.context: Current SECTION_TARGETS at skills-and-hooks.ts:224 points to `.cursor/rules` as a file path. Real Cursor projects use `.cursor/rules/<name>.mdc` directory with per-rule .mdc files containing YAML front-matter. The orphan template cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc already demonstrates the .mdc YAML pattern (alwaysApply: true). Fixing this is in-scope for rc.19 consolidation since rc.19 is rebuilding the Cursor bootstrap surface anyway.
options:
Fix: write to `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` with `---
alwaysApply: true
description: Fabric Protocol bootstrap rules
---

` prefix before managed block (proper Cursor convention)
Keep: write to `.cursor/rules` as single file (matches current tests, but stale Cursor convention)
Defer: leave SECTION_TARGETS unchanged in rc.19; file a separate rc.20 ticket for Cursor target fix

clarification_needs.question: Should rc.19 keep dual-marker support in uninstall regex (strip BOTH `fabric:knowledge-base` AND `fabric:bootstrap`) for rc.18→rc.19 migration, or clean-slate (rc.19 uninstall only strips `fabric:bootstrap`)?
clarification_needs.context: Per memory project_v2_rc_continuation.md, v2.0 is never published, so zero real users exist. Per memory feedback_clean_slate.md, prefer clean-slate refactors. However, internal dev workflows (this very repo's AGENTS.md L10-L21) carry the rc.18 `fabric:knowledge-base` marker. If rc.19 uninstall doesn't recognize the old marker, dev cleanup will leave orphan blocks.
options: Clean-slate: rc.19 strips only `fabric:bootstrap` marker; manual migration of any existing rc.18 blocks (regex find-replace by hand)
Dual-marker support: uninstall regex matches both `fabric:knowledge-base` AND `fabric:bootstrap` for one RC cycle; remove in rc.20
Pre-migration script: rc.19 install runs a one-time marker rename pass before the standard install logic; uninstall stays clean-slate

clarification_needs.question: Should the new managed-block body be (a) a byte-copy of `.fabric/AGENTS.md` + optional `\n---\n` + `.fabric/project-rules.md`, or (b) the byte-copy plus the existing fabric_language language line + structural markers?
clarification_needs.context: The locked .fabric/AGENTS.md content already covers behavior rules + KB section but does NOT mention fabric_language explicitly. The current rc.18 managed block ends with `- **Language**: rendered per fabric_language in .fabric/fabric-config.json (current: <value>)`. If the rc.19 body is a pure byte-copy, the fabric_language signal disappears. If it's byte-copy plus tail, the source-of-truth claim (`.fabric/AGENTS.md` IS the bootstrap) is diluted.
options: Pure byte-copy: managed block = `.fabric/AGENTS.md` content verbatim + optional `\n---\n` + `.fabric/project-rules.md`. fabric_language goes elsewhere (e.g., a comment line in AGENTS.md itself, or surfaced via a separate scan recommendation)

Byte-copy + tail: managed block = AGENTS.md content + optional separator + project-rules.md + auto-appended `- Language: <fabric_language>` bullet. Preserves rc.18 signal but adds non-AGENTS.md content to the block
Byte-copy with placeholder: .fabric/AGENTS.md contains a literal `{{fabric_language}}` placeholder that the writer interpolates. Single source, dynamic value, no tail-append

clarification_needs.question: Should `.fabric/project-rules.md` be created as an empty file by `fab install` (with a TODO marker), created only on demand (`fab knowledge add --as-project-rules`), or never auto-created (skip in block when absent)?
clarification_needs.context: The locked .fabric/AGENTS.md content doesn't mention project-rules.md, but the rc.19 brief mentions optional `\n---\n` + `.fabric/project-rules.md` as the block body. The brief also asks whether 'fab install should skip when absent.' Three install behaviors differ in user-friction and surface area.
options: Create-on-install: `fab install` writes `.fabric/project-rules.md` with a TODO header if missing (parallels AGENTS.md creation at install.ts:730-732). Pro: discoverable. Con: pollutes fresh installs with empty file

Skip-when-absent: block writer reads project-rules.md only if existsSync; absent → block contains only AGENTS.md content. User creates project-rules.md manually when needed. Pro: clean fresh install. Con: less discoverable
Wizard-prompted: install wizard asks 'Add project-specific rules file?' (Y/N); if Y, create with TODO scaffold. Pro: opt-in discovery. Con: more wizard steps

clarification_needs.question: What should the root `CLAUDE.md` thin shell contain (assuming the install wizard creates it for Claude users)?
clarification_needs.context: Three options for Claude Code: (a) NO root CLAUDE.md — rely on Claude reading root AGENTS.md natively. (b) Root CLAUDE.md with just `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` import. (c) Root CLAUDE.md with managed block (same as AGENTS.md). Claude Code prefers CLAUDE.md when present and falls back to AGENTS.md. Two-file maintenance creates drift risk if both carry the block.
options: No root CLAUDE.md: skip; AGENTS.md is the single source. Pro: no drift. Con: Claude users may expect CLAUDE.md

Thin import: write CLAUDE.md with just `# Project Knowledge

@.fabric/AGENTS.md
@.fabric/project-rules.md` (no managed block). Pro: no drift, explicit imports. Con: project-rules.md may not exist

Mirror AGENTS.md: write CLAUDE.md with the SAME managed block as AGENTS.md (writer fans out). Pro: Claude sees content even without @-import support. Con: two files to keep in sync (managed block handles this idempotently)

_metadata.task_description: rc.19 bootstrap consolidation — replace three drifted bootstrap templates with single canonical .fabric/AGENTS.md source; find dead-pointer references to clean.
